_comment: Institutional managers making directional AI infrastructure bets — compute, power, semiconductors, data centers. Trimmed to signal-only: no passive index giants.
_theme: AGI / AI infrastructure investing — compute, power, semiconductors, data centers, and picks-and-shovels plays.

agi_thesis._note: Funds built explicitly around the AGI/AI infrastructure thesis. Highest signal.

managers.name: Situational Awareness LP
managers.notes: Leopold Aschenbrenner and Carl Shulman. Fund named after Aschenbrenner's AGI essay. Thesis is explicitly about AGI timelines driving compute, power, and semiconductor demand. As of Q1 2026: $8.5B notional put exposure against AI chip stocks, large long in Bloom Energy. Watching the energy-for-AI angle directly.

ai_infrastructure_concentrated._note: Hedge funds with explicit, concentrated bets on AI infrastructure — chips, cloud, data centers. Their position changes are high-conviction signals.

managers.name: Altimeter Capital Management LLC
managers.notes: Brad Gerstner. One of the loudest AI infrastructure bulls on Wall Street. Has publicly argued AI capex is underspent. Heavy in hyperscalers, data center plays, and compute. Highly concentrated — position changes matter.

managers.name: Coatue Management LLC
managers.notes: Philippe Laffont. Deep semiconductor and hyperscaler bets. Known for early and large NVIDIA position. AI infrastructure is core to the portfolio thesis, not incidental.

managers.name: Whale Rock Capital Management LLC
managers.notes: Concentrated long/short tech fund. Known for one of the largest NVIDIA positions among hedge funds. Cloud and AI infrastructure are primary exposures.

managers.name: ARK Investment Management LLC
managers.notes: Cathie Wood. Explicit AI, robotics, and energy storage thesis. Highly transparent — ARK publishes daily trades. Useful for tracking retail-facing AI infrastructure sentiment and early-stage bets.

tech_growth_heavy_ai._note: Growth-focused hedge funds without an explicit AGI thesis but with heavy, active AI infrastructure exposure. Their moves reflect sophisticated tech research, not index mechanics.

managers.name: Tiger Global Management LLC
managers.notes: Chase Coleman. One of the most influential tech funds globally. Active position management across hyperscalers, semiconductors, and AI software. Not a pure AI thesis but AI is the dominant theme in the portfolio.

managers.name: D1 Capital Partners LP
managers.notes: Dan Sundheim (Viking alum). Concentrated growth equity. Heavy AI/cloud exposure. High conviction, low turnover — position changes are meaningful signals.

managers.name: Dragoneer Investment Group LLC
managers.notes: Growth fund concentrated in cloud, AI infrastructure software, and data platforms. Watches the software layer of AI infrastructure closely.

managers.name: Baillie Gifford & Co
managers.notes: Scottish long-duration growth manager. Famous for early NVIDIA, Tesla, and AI bets. Very low turnover — when they buy or sell, it reflects a multi-year thesis change, not short-term trading.
